NEW VARIABLE
Create a Variable
Choose the scope. Global variables live in the Universal Catalog and are available everywhere. Policy variables belong to a specific policy.
📄
Raw / Catalog Variable
A data field from a source system (LOS, Bureau, BSA…). Added to the Universal Catalog, available in every policy.
e.g. gst_filing_ratio, rc_verification_status
✓
ƒ
Policy Variable (Derived)
Computed or derived within a specific policy — formula, aggregation, entity mapping, or CQB pipeline.
e.g. foir = obligations / income, enq_l6m_count
✓
🔍
Search Results
Data Source Legend
LOS Loan Origination System — application data captured at entry
Bureau Credit bureau pull — CIBIL, Experian, CRIF, Equifax
BSA Bank Statement Analysis — 6/12M transaction data
AA Account Aggregator — RBI AA framework data
FIN Financial Statements — ITR, P&L, Balance Sheet
GST GST Portal — GSTR filings, turnover, compliance
EXT External API — third-party enrichment services
INT Internal — in-house derived or system-generated fields
Create a Variable
Raw / Catalog Variable — a field sourced from an external system (LOS, Bureau, BSA, GST…). Defined once, reusable across all policies.
Policy Variable — a value derived or computed within a specific policy (FOIR formula, bureau aggregation, CQB pipeline).
📄
Raw / Catalog Variable
A data field that comes directly from a source system. You define its name, data type, source (LOS / Bureau / BSA…), and which products it applies to.
You define
Name • Label • Data Type • Source • Sub-source • DB Table • Applicable Products
e.g. gst_turnover_annual, rc_verification_status
Define Raw Variable →
ƒ
Policy Variable
A derived or computed value created within a specific policy — a formula, aggregation, entity mapping, or multi-step data pipeline. Lives inside one policy's workspace.
You define
Formula / Aggregation / Conditional / Entity Mapping / CQB Query
e.g. foir = obligations / income, enq_l6m_count
Select Policy & Create →
Not sure which to choose?  If the value comes from an external system as-is (LOS application data, a bureau score, a bank balance) → Raw Variable. If you need to calculate it from other fields (FOIR, LTV, max bureau score across entities) → Policy Variable.
Define a Raw / Catalog Variable
This field will be added to the Universal Catalog and available across all policies
lowercase, underscores only
Added instantly to the Universal Catalog — available in every policy's variable picker
Select a Policy
The computed / derived variable will be created inside this policy's workspace

Create an Entity Variable
Map a catalog variable to a specific party (co-applicant, guarantor, director). Each entity gets its own auto-named variable.

Add the non-applicant parties for this policy

Batch Test — Policy Stress Testing
Run up to 50 borrower profiles through the active policy. Use the pre-loaded test suite or upload your own CSV.
📄
🔎 Policy Calibration
Diagnose why approval rate is where it is and find the highest-value threshold adjustment to close the gap.
%
Select a product and policy to simulate.
Input Values
Computed Values

Threshold Sensitivity
A/B Testing — Champion vs Challenger
Synthesized live experiment. Configure your segment, run both policies, get realistic performance metrics as if deployed to live traffic.
1Select Policies
2Filter Set
3Sample Mode
4Expected Outcome
▲ Champion — Current Baseline
△ Challenger — Proposed New
70% / 30%
Define Population Filter
Build conditions to define which applications are included in this experiment. Uses the same condition engine as Rule Builder.
Sample Mode
Choose how many cases from the filtered set to include in the experiment.
Expected Outcome Filter
Optionally focus the experiment on cases with a specific expected outcome.
Configure steps 1–3 to see experiment summary.
Multi-BRE — Multi-Lender Decisioning
Register lenders with their policies, quotas and appetite. Run borrower profiles or batches across all lenders simultaneously to find best-fit matches.
Define lenders and link them to products & policies. Set priority order, quota and risk appetite.
New Lender
No lenders registered yet. Click "+ Add Lender" to begin.
Select Run Mode
◆
Best Match
Single borrower profile → ranked list of approving lenders
☰
Ranked Grid
Batch of borrowers × all lenders → eligibility matrix
⟶
Optimal Assignment
Assign each borrower to best lender respecting quotas & priority

Governance & Risk Controls
Performance analytics, audit trail, maker-checker, scorecard engine, negative lists, and API spec.
🎯
Scorecard Engine has moved
The Scorecard Engine is now a dedicated tab in the main navigation, positioned correctly in the decision pipeline — after Rule Builder and before Simulate.
Negative List Management
Blacklists and exclusion lists checked before policy evaluation. Hard blocks = immediate decline.
Add Exclusion Entry
TypeValueReasonSeverityAddedBy

Rule Template Library
Click a template to add it to the sub-category
Test Case Library — Regression Suite
Named, tagged test cases. Run selected cases against any policy. See which cases regress when you change a rule.
Tags:SMOKEMust always passREGRESSIONPreviously failed — verify fix holdsEDGEBoundary profiles
⚠ Regression Alert: Red banner fires if any previously-passing case now fails after a rule change.
Test Cases 0 selected
Select test cases and click Run to see results.
Rule Conflict & Coverage Analyzer
Scans all active rules for conflicts, redundancies, dead rules, and coverage gaps. Run after any policy change.
⚡ Conflict
Two rules that fire with contradictory outcomes
♻ Redundancy
A rule fully covered by another — will never uniquely fire
💀 Dead Rule
Condition that can never be true given variable range
◯ Coverage Gap
A numeric range with no rule — inputs pass unchecked
Select a policy and click Run Analysis to scan for issues.
🤖
AI Rule Assist
Describe a rule in English → structured condition
Quick Templates
🤖 AI Rule Assist
Describe a lending policy rule in plain English and I'll convert it to a structured condition tree.

"CIBIL score must be at least 700"
"FOIR should not exceed 50%"
"Decline if any write-offs in last 24 months"
⚠
Switch to Production?
This affects your live underwriting policies
In Production mode:
• Any rule or policy changes will affect live loan decisions
• Deployed policies will be called by the LOS immediately
• Changes require Maker-Checker approval before activating
• All actions are logged in the audit trail
Type PRODUCTION below to confirm:
⚠ PRODUCTION ENVIRONMENT — Changes to rules and policies affect live loan decisions. Use with care.
